From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American multidisciplinary artist

**Helga Davis** is a New York-based multidisciplinary artist who works as an actress, singer, writer and composer, as well as a radio and podcast host.[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1)

## Performances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helga_Davis&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Performances")]

Helga Davis performed as a principal actor in the 25th-anniversary international revival of [Robert Wilson](/wiki/Robert_Wilson_(director) "Robert Wilson (director)") and [Philip Glass](/wiki/Philip_Glass "Philip Glass")'s opera *[Einstein on the Beach](/wiki/Einstein_on_the_Beach "Einstein on the Beach").* *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* described Davis' performance as "Compelling."[[2]](#cite_note-2) Robert Wilson described Davis as "a united whole, with spellbinding inner power and strength."[[3]](#cite_note-auto2-3) David Keenan, of *[The Wire](/wiki/The_Wire_(magazine) "The Wire (magazine)")* magazine, described Davis as "a powerful vocalist with an almost operatic range and all the bruised sensuality of Jeanne Lee."[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Davis' music career has included a stint in the rock band Women in Love, in the 1990s.[[5]](#cite_note-auto1-5) More recently, Davis has starred in operas and theater pieces internationally, including Robert Wilson's *The Temptation of St. Anthony,* libretto and score by [Bernice Johnson Reagon](/wiki/Bernice_Johnson_Reagon "Bernice Johnson Reagon"); her and [Toshi Reagon](/wiki/Toshi_Reagon "Toshi Reagon")'s *[Octavia E. Butler](/wiki/Octavia_E._Butler "Octavia E. Butler")'s Parable Of The Sower,* *Milton* by Katie Pearl and Lisa Damour; *The Blue Planet,* a multimedia theater piece, by [Peter Greenaway](/wiki/Peter_Greenaway "Peter Greenaway") and [Saskia Boddeke](/wiki/Saskia_Boddeke "Saskia Boddeke"); and Soho Rep's *Jomama Jones, Radiate,* which was included in *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* theatre critic Hilton Als’s top ten list.[[3]](#cite_note-auto2-3) Among the many works that have been written for Davis are *Faust's Box,* written and directed by Italian contemporary music composer [Andrea Liberovici](/wiki/Andrea_Liberovici "Andrea Liberovici"); *Oceanic Verses* by [Paola Prestini](/wiki/Paola_Prestini "Paola Prestini"), with libretto by Donna DiNovelli; and *Elsewhere* by [Missy Mazzoli](/wiki/Missy_Mazzoli "Missy Mazzoli") and [Maya Beiser](/wiki/Maya_Beiser "Maya Beiser").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Davis hosts the "Helga" podcast, live events for New Sounds and [WQXR-FM](/wiki/WQXR-FM "WQXR-FM")'s Q2 Music.[[3]](#cite_note-auto2-3)

## Awards and appointments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helga_Davis&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Awards and appointments")]

Davis is the recipient of the BRIC Fireworks grant and the ASCAP Multimedia Award, as well as the 2019 Greenfield Prize.[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1) Davis was appointed the Visiting Curator for the Performing Arts at the [Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum](/wiki/Isabella_Stewart_Gardner_Museum "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum"), in Boston, for 2018-2021.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helga_Davis&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Davis was born and raised in [Harlem](/wiki/Harlem "Harlem"), in New York City.[[8]](#cite_note-8) She performed in high school theater productions, studied piano and sang in a church choir.[[5]](#cite_note-auto1-5)
